ucs.title: [LLM] Embargo / MDeployer safe-mode reboot to bypass EDR before encryption (BlackCat-lineage tradecraft)
ucs.description: Hunts Embargo's signature pre-encryption reboot-into-safe-mode chain (inherited from the BlackCat/ALPHV codebase) where the actor sets `safeboot` via bcdedit or registry, registers an auto-start RunOnce/SafeBoot entry pointing at MDeployer or the Rust encryptor, and forces a reboot — booting the host into Minimal/Network safe mode where Defender, MDE, and most EDR drivers do not load.
ucs.splunk: | tstats summariesonly=true count min(_time) as firstTime max(_time) as lastTime values(Processes.process) as cmd values(Processes.parent_process_name) as parent values(Processes.user) as user from datamodel=Endpoint.Processes where (Processes.process_name=bcdedit.exe Processes.process IN ("*safeboot*minimal*","*safeboot*network*")) OR (Processes.process_name=reg.exe Processes.process="*\\CurrentControlSet\\Control\\SafeBoot\\*" Processes.process="*add*") OR (Processes.process_name IN (powershell.exe,pwsh.exe) Processes.process IN ("*Set-ItemProperty*SafeBoot*","*New-ItemProperty*SafeBoot*","*bcdedit*safeboot*")) by host Processes.dest Processes.user Processes.process_name _time span=5m | `drop_dm_object_name(Processes)` | rename dest as host_dest | join type=inner host [ | tstats summariesonly=true count values(Processes.process) as shutdown_cmd from datamodel=Endpoint.Processes where Processes.process_name IN (shutdown.exe,cmd.exe,powershell.exe) Processes.process IN ("*shutdown*/r*","*shutdown*-r*","*Restart-Computer*") by host _time span=15m ] | where _time<=lastTime+900 AND _time>=firstTime-60 | eval signal="Embargo safe-mode reboot chain" | table firstTime lastTime host user process_name cmd shutdown_cmd signal | `security_content_ctime(firstTime)` | `security_content_ctime(lastTime)`
ucs.kql:
// Embargo / MDeployer safe-mode reboot chain — bcdedit safeboot OR SafeBoot registry add, then forced reboot within 15 min on the same host
let Lookback = 7d;
let WindowMin = 15m;
let SafeBootStaging = DeviceProcessEvents
    | where Timestamp > ago(Lookback)
    | where AccountName !endswith "$"
    | where (FileName =~ "bcdedit.exe"
              and ProcessCommandLine has "safeboot"
              and ProcessCommandLine has_any ("minimal", "network"))
         or (FileName =~ "reg.exe"
              and ProcessCommandLine has "add"
              and ProcessCommandLine has @"\Control\SafeBoot\")
         or (FileName in~ ("powershell.exe", "pwsh.exe")
              and ProcessCommandLine has_any ("Set-ItemProperty", "New-ItemProperty", "bcdedit")
              and ProcessCommandLine has "SafeBoot")
    | project SafeBootTime = Timestamp, DeviceId, DeviceName, AccountName,
              StagingProcess = FileName,
              StagingCmd = ProcessCommandLine,
              StagingParent = InitiatingProcessFileName,
              StagingParentCmd = InitiatingProcessCommandLine;
// Reboot trigger on the SAME host shortly after
DeviceProcessEvents
| where Timestamp > ago(Lookback)
| where AccountName !endswith "$"
| where (FileName =~ "shutdown.exe" and ProcessCommandLine has_any ("/r", "-r"))
     or (FileName in~ ("powershell.exe", "pwsh.exe") and ProcessCommandLine has "Restart-Computer")
     or (FileName =~ "cmd.exe" and ProcessCommandLine has_all ("shutdown", "/r"))
| join kind=inner SafeBootStaging on DeviceId
| where Timestamp between (SafeBootTime .. SafeBootTime + WindowMin)
| extend DelaySec = datetime_diff('second', Timestamp, SafeBootTime)
| project SafeBootTime, RebootTime = Timestamp, DelaySec,
          DeviceName, AccountName,
          StagingProcess, StagingCmd, StagingParent, StagingParentCmd,
          RebootProcess = FileName, RebootCmd = ProcessCommandLine
| order by SafeBootTime desc
ucs.sentinel_kql:
// Embargo / MDeployer safe-mode reboot chain — Sentinel SecurityEvent (4688) port
let Lookback = 7d;
let WindowMin = 15m;
let SafeBootStaging = SecurityEvent
    | where TimeGenerated > ago(Lookback)
    | where EventID == 4688
    | where Account !endswith "$"
    | where (NewProcessName endswith "\\bcdedit.exe"
              and CommandLine has "safeboot"
              and CommandLine has_any ("minimal", "network"))
         or (NewProcessName endswith "\\reg.exe"
              and CommandLine has "add"
              and CommandLine has @"\Control\SafeBoot\")
         or (NewProcessName has_any ("powershell.exe", "pwsh.exe")
              and CommandLine has_any ("Set-ItemProperty", "New-ItemProperty", "bcdedit")
              and CommandLine has "SafeBoot")
    | project SafeBootTime = TimeGenerated, Computer, Account,
              StagingProcess = NewProcessName,
              StagingCmd = CommandLine,
              StagingParent = ParentProcessName;
SecurityEvent
| where TimeGenerated > ago(Lookback)
| where EventID == 4688
| where Account !endswith "$"
| where (NewProcessName endswith "\\shutdown.exe" and CommandLine has_any ("/r", "-r"))
     or (NewProcessName has_any ("powershell.exe", "pwsh.exe") and CommandLine has "Restart-Computer")
     or (NewProcessName endswith "\\cmd.exe" and CommandLine has_all ("shutdown", "/r"))
| join kind=inner SafeBootStaging on Computer
| where TimeGenerated between (SafeBootTime .. SafeBootTime + WindowMin)
| extend DelaySec = datetime_diff('second', TimeGenerated, SafeBootTime)
| project SafeBootTime, RebootTime = TimeGenerated, DelaySec,
          Computer, Account,
          StagingProcess, StagingCmd, StagingParent,
          RebootProcess = NewProcessName, RebootCmd = CommandLine
| order by SafeBootTime desc
ucs.sigma_yaml:
title: Embargo Ransomware Safe-Mode Reboot Staging
id: 8c5f7b2e-1d3a-4e2c-9b48-emba01safemode
status: experimental
description: Detects bcdedit / registry modification to enable Windows Safe Boot, a known precursor to Embargo (BlackCat-lineage) ransomware encryption to bypass EDR drivers.
author: SOC Detection Eng
references:
  - https://www.welivesecurity.com/en/eset-research/embargo-ransomware-rock-n-rust/
logsource:
  category: process_creation
  product: windows
detection:
  selection_bcdedit:
    Image|endswith: '\bcdedit.exe'
    CommandLine|contains|all:
      - 'safeboot'
    CommandLine|contains:
      - 'minimal'
      - 'network'
  selection_reg:
    Image|endswith: '\reg.exe'
    CommandLine|contains|all:
      - 'add'
      - '\Control\SafeBoot\'
  selection_ps:
    Image|endswith:
      - '\powershell.exe'
      - '\pwsh.exe'
    CommandLine|contains: 'SafeBoot'
  filter_user:
    User|endswith: '$'
  condition: (selection_bcdedit or selection_reg or selection_ps) and not filter_user
fields:
  - Image
  - CommandLine
  - ParentImage
  - User
falsepositives:
  - Legitimate troubleshooting by IT admins
  - Windows Recovery / Imaging tools
level: high
tags:
  - attack.defense_evasion
  - attack.t1562.009
  - attack.impact
  - attack.t1486
ucs.rationale: Reboot-into-safe-mode is one of the few hard pivots that uniquely identifies Embargo and its BlackCat/ALPHV ancestor — REvil and Conti did not do it, and most modern crypters skip it. Embargo's MDeployer .NET loader specifically registers a SafeBoot RunOnce entry pointing at the encryptor, then issues `shutdown /r /t 0`. Pairing the two events on the same host within 15 minutes — both attributable
_sigma_issues: sigma parse error: Sigma rule identifier must be an UUID

ucs.title: [LLM] Embargo MS4Killer BYOVD — vulnerable kernel driver service install + EDR process termination
ucs.description: Hunts Embargo's MS4Killer (a.k.a. Probably-Killer / 'msdrv'-style) Bring-Your-Own-Vulnerable-Driver tooling: a kernel driver dropped to a non-system path (Temp / Public / ProgramData), registered as a service, then used (via IOCTL) to terminate Defender/EDR user-mode processes within minutes — the technique Embargo inherits from the BlackCat affiliate toolkit.
ucs.splunk: | tstats summariesonly=true count min(_time) as firstTime max(_time) as lastTime values(Filesystem.file_path) as file_path values(Filesystem.file_name) as file_name from datamodel=Endpoint.Filesystem where Filesystem.file_name="*.sys" Filesystem.file_path IN ("*\\Temp\\*","*\\Users\\Public\\*","*\\ProgramData\\*","*\\AppData\\Local\\Temp\\*") by host Filesystem.user _time span=10m | `drop_dm_object_name(Filesystem)` | join type=inner host [ | tstats summariesonly=true count values(Processes.process) as kill_cmds values(Processes.process_name) as killer_proc from datamodel=Endpoint.Processes where (Processes.process_name IN (taskkill.exe,sc.exe,net.exe,powershell.exe,pwsh.exe,wmic.exe) AND Processes.process IN ("*MsMpEng*","*MsSense*","*SenseIR*","*WinDefend*","*Sense*","*CSFalconService*","*xagt*","*SentinelAgent*","*ekrn*","*mfeesp*","*MBAMService*")) OR (Processes.parent_process_name=services.exe AND Processes.process IN ("*\\Temp\\*.sys*","*\\ProgramData\\*.sys*","*\\Public\\*.sys*")) by host _time span=30m ] | where lastTime>=firstTime AND (lastTime-firstTime)<1800 | eval signal="Embargo MS4Killer BYOVD candidate" | table firstTime lastTime host user file_name file_path killer_proc kill_cmds signal | `security_content_ctime(firstTime)` | `security_content_ctime(lastTime)`
ucs.kql:
// Embargo MS4Killer BYOVD — non-system-path .sys dropped, kernel driver service installed, then EDR/AV processes killed on same host within 30 min
let Lookback = 7d;
let WindowMin = 30m;
let EdrTargets = dynamic([
    "MsMpEng.exe", "MsSense.exe", "SenseIR.exe", "SenseCncProxy.exe", "WinDefend",
    "CSFalconService.exe", "CSFalconContainer.exe", "xagt.exe", "SentinelAgent.exe",
    "SentinelServiceHost.exe", "ekrn.exe", "egui.exe", "mfeesp.exe", "masvc.exe",
    "MBAMService.exe", "AvastSvc.exe", "avp.exe", "sophosfs.exe", "SAVService.exe",
    "TmCCSF.exe", "PccNTMon.exe"
  ]);
let KnownVulnerableDrivers = dynamic([
    "truesight.sys", "gmer.sys", "viragt64.sys", "procexp152.sys", "procexp.sys",
    "rentdrv2.sys", "dbutil_2_3.sys", "dbutildrv2.sys", "ene.sys", "asrdrv101.sys",
    "asrdrv102.sys", "asrdrv103.sys", "WnBios.sys", "WnBios64.sys", "mhyprot2.sys",
    "daxin.sys", "kavg.sys", "ms4killer.sys"
  ]);
let DriverDrop = DeviceFileEvents
    | where Timestamp > ago(Lookback)
    | where ActionType in ("FileCreated", "FileRenamed")
    | where FileName endswith ".sys"
    | where FolderPath has_any (@"\Temp\", @"\AppData\Local\Temp\", @"\Users\Public\",
                                 @"\ProgramData\", @"\Downloads\", @"\PerfLogs\")
        or FileName in~ (KnownVulnerableDrivers)
    | where InitiatingProcessAccountName !endswith "$"
    | project DropTime = Timestamp, DeviceId, DeviceName,
              DroppedDriver = FileName, DropPath = FolderPath, DropSHA256 = SHA256,
              DropperProcess = InitiatingProcessFileName,
              DropperCmd = InitiatingProcessCommandLine,
              DropperUser = InitiatingProcessAccountName;
// Service registration for that driver (HKLM\SYSTEM\CurrentControlSet\Services\<name>\ImagePath)
let ServiceReg = DeviceRegistryEvents
    | where Timestamp > ago(Lookback)
    | where ActionType in ("RegistryValueSet", "RegistryKeyCreated")
    | where RegistryKey has @"\CurrentControlSet\Services\"
    | where RegistryValueName =~ "ImagePath"
    | where RegistryValueData has_any (@"\Temp\", @"\Public\", @"\ProgramData\",
                                        @"\AppData\Local\Temp\", @"\Downloads\")
    | where RegistryValueData has ".sys"
    | project SvcRegTime = Timestamp, DeviceId, DeviceName,
              SvcKey = RegistryKey, SvcImagePath = tostring(RegistryValueData),
              SvcRegisterer = InitiatingProcessFileName,
              SvcRegistererCmd = InitiatingProcessCommandLine;
// EDR/AV process termination on same host
let EdrKill = DeviceProcessEvents
    | where Timestamp > ago(Lookback)
    | where (FileName in~ ("taskkill.exe", "sc.exe", "net.exe", "wmic.exe",
                            "powershell.exe", "pwsh.exe")
              and (ProcessCommandLine has_any (EdrTargets)
                   or ProcessCommandLine has_any ("WinDefend", "Sense", "MDE", "Defender")))
    | project KillTime = Timestamp, DeviceId, DeviceName, AccountName,
              KillProcess = FileName, KillCmd = ProcessCommandLine,
              KillParent = InitiatingProcessFileName;
DriverDrop
| join kind=inner ServiceReg on DeviceId
| where SvcRegTime between (DropTime .. DropTime + WindowMin)
| join kind=inner EdrKill on DeviceId
| where KillTime between (DropTime .. DropTime + WindowMin)
| project DropTime, SvcRegTime, KillTime,
          DeviceName, DropperUser,
          DroppedDriver, DropPath, DropSHA256,
          DropperProcess, DropperCmd,
          SvcImagePath, SvcRegisterer,
          KillProcess, KillCmd
| order by DropTime desc
ucs.sentinel_kql:
// Embargo MS4Killer BYOVD — Sentinel ASIM port (ImFileEvent + ImRegistryEvent + ImProcessCreate)
let Lookback = 7d;
let WindowMin = 30m;
let EdrTargets = dynamic([
    "MsMpEng", "MsSense", "SenseIR", "SenseCncProxy", "WinDefend",
    "CSFalconService", "xagt", "SentinelAgent", "ekrn", "mfeesp",
    "MBAMService", "AvastSvc", "avp", "sophosfs", "TmCCSF"
  ]);
let KnownVulnerableDrivers = dynamic([
    "truesight.sys", "gmer.sys", "viragt64.sys", "procexp152.sys",
    "rentdrv2.sys", "dbutil_2_3.sys", "dbutildrv2.sys", "ene.sys",
    "asrdrv101.sys", "WnBios.sys", "mhyprot2.sys", "ms4killer.sys"
  ]);
let DriverDrop = ImFileEvent
    | where TimeGenerated > ago(Lookback)
    | where EventType in ("FileCreated", "FileRenamed")
    | where TargetFileName endswith ".sys"
    | where TargetFilePath has_any (@"\Temp\", @"\AppData\Local\Temp\",
                                     @"\Users\Public\", @"\ProgramData\", @"\Downloads\")
        or TargetFileName in~ (KnownVulnerableDrivers)
    | where ActorUsername !endswith "$"
    | project DropTime = TimeGenerated, DvcHostname,
              DroppedDriver = TargetFileName, DropPath = TargetFilePath,
              DropSHA256 = TargetFileSHA256,
              DropperProcess = ActingProcessName,
              DropperCmd = ActingProcessCommandLine,
              DropperUser = ActorUsername;
let ServiceReg = ImRegistryEvent
    | where TimeGenerated > ago(Lookback)
    | where RegistryKey has @"\CurrentControlSet\Services\"
    | where RegistryValue =~ "ImagePath"
    | where tostring(RegistryValueData) has_any (@"\Temp\", @"\Public\",
                                                  @"\ProgramData\", @"\Downloads\")
    | where tostring(RegistryValueData) has ".sys"
    | project SvcRegTime = TimeGenerated, DvcHostname,
              SvcKey = RegistryKey,
              SvcImagePath = tostring(RegistryValueData),
              SvcRegisterer = ActingProcessName;
let EdrKill = ImProcessCreate
    | where TimeGenerated > ago(Lookback)
    | where TargetProcessName has_any ("taskkill.exe", "sc.exe", "net.exe", "wmic.exe",
                                         "powershell.exe", "pwsh.exe")
    | where TargetProcessCommandLine has_any (EdrTargets)
         or TargetProcessCommandLine has_any ("WinDefend", "Sense", "Defender")
    | project KillTime = TimeGenerated, DvcHostname, ActorUsername,
              KillProcess = TargetProcessName,
              KillCmd = TargetProcessCommandLine;
DriverDrop
| join kind=inner ServiceReg on DvcHostname
| where SvcRegTime between (DropTime .. DropTime + WindowMin)
| join kind=inner EdrKill on DvcHostname
| where KillTime between (DropTime .. DropTime + WindowMin)
| project DropTime, SvcRegTime, KillTime,
          DvcHostname, DropperUser,
          DroppedDriver, DropPath, DropSHA256,
          DropperProcess, DropperCmd,
          SvcImagePath, SvcRegisterer,
          KillProcess, KillCmd
| order by DropTime desc
ucs.rationale: Embargo's distinguishing tooling is the Rust-based MS4Killer (a.k.a. 'Probably-Killer' / 'MS4'), which ESET and TrendMicro have publicly attributed to the group as inherited from the BlackCat/ALPHV affiliate kit. Its operational signature — drop a vulnerable signed driver (often impersonating Defender by name 'MS4Killer.sys'), register it via Service Control Manager from a non-system path, then ca
